personas.name: Consistent Mood Journal User
personas.description: A user who wants to build a steady habit of checking in with their emotions, answering guided questions, and writing short journal entries so they can better understand how they feel over time.
goals: Track moods regularly
Write down thoughts in a simple way
Build a daily or weekly self-reflection habit
Look back at past entries to notice patterns
pain_points: Can lose motivation if the habit feels hard to maintain
Wants more flexibility in how emotions are recorded
Needs the app to make tracking feel simple and worth returning to
context: Uses the app during the day to log moods and thoughts
Often depends on prompts or reminders to keep the habit going
May be new to journaling and wants more structure than a blank notebook
constraints: The check-in process should be quick and easy to repeat
Past entries should remain available for later review
The app should support regular use without feeling overwhelming

personas.name: Therapy Support User
personas.description: A user who uses MindDoc as a support tool alongside therapy, counselling, or doctor visits, and wants help organizing symptoms, feelings, and patterns before speaking to a professional.
goals: Track symptoms and mood changes over time
Bring useful notes to therapy or doctor appointments
Better explain emotions and patterns to a professional
Feel more prepared during mental health discussions
pain_points: Can struggle to remember important feelings or patterns later
Needs notes and summaries that are actually useful in appointments
Wants the app to support care without replacing professional help
context: Uses the app between therapy sessions or appointments
May have started using the app because it was recommended by a doctor or therapist
Looks at past check-ins to prepare for conversations about mental health
constraints: Information should be easy to review before appointments
Tracked data should feel trustworthy and organized
The app should support, not interfere with, professional care

personas.name: Cost-Conscious User
personas.description: A user who is interested in improving their mental health but is very sensitive to pricing, and becomes frustrated when key features, reports, or helpful resources are locked behind a subscription.
goals: Understand what the app offers before paying
Access useful mental health tools at a reasonable cost
Use important features without feeling misled
Decide clearly whether the app is worth subscribing to
pain_points: Feels disappointed when too much of the app is paywalled
May feel the app is not useful enough in the free version
Can see the app as unfair or exploitative if pricing is unclear
context: Usually starts with the free version before deciding whether to pay
May compare MindDoc with cheaper or fully free alternatives
Often evaluates value very early in the user experience
constraints: Pricing and locked features should be communicated clearly
The free experience should still provide some meaningful value
Subscription decisions should not depend on hidden limitations

personas.name: Insight-Seeking User
personas.description: A user who wants the app to provide meaningful insights, reports, and feedback based on their answers, and expects those outputs to feel relevant, helpful, and more than just obvious statements.
goals: Receive useful insight from check-ins and questions
Understand patterns in mood and mental health over time
Get reports or feedback that feel personalized
Learn something valuable from using the app regularly
pain_points: Gets frustrated when reports feel shallow or obvious
May lose interest if questions feel repetitive
Wants feedback that clearly matches their input and progress
context: Uses the app over time and expects the quality of feedback to improve with more data
Pays attention to summaries, reports, and in-app guidance
Often judges the app by whether it gives real value beyond mood logging
constraints: Reports should reflect the user’s recorded data in a meaningful way
Feedback should be understandable and relevant to the user’s responses
Insights should feel useful enough to justify continued use

personas.name: Reminder-Dependent User
personas.description: A user who depends on notifications and reminders to remember check-ins, and needs those reminders to arrive consistently and at times that are practical instead of distracting or easy to miss.
goals: Remember to complete regular mood check-ins
Stay engaged with the app through helpful reminders
Receive notifications at useful times
Avoid missing entries because reminders are unreliable
pain_points: May miss check-ins if notifications do not arrive
Gets annoyed when reminder timing feels random or inconvenient
Can feel overwhelmed if the app sends too many reminders
context: Uses reminders as a main trigger to open the app
May already struggle with energy, mood, or routine, so external prompts matter a lot
Notices reminder quality quickly during the first few days or weeks of use
constraints: Reminder timing should be manageable for the user
Reminder frequency should support use without becoming excessive
